title: RNA Precipitation Protocol
author: Liz Hughes
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.73chqiw

steps.title: Make up the volume of your...
steps.instruction: Make up the volume of your RNA solution to 500μl with RNase free water.

steps.title: Add 50 μl of room temperature...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of room temperature 3M Sodium Acetate (pH 5.2-5.5)

steps.title: Add 500 μl of room temperature Propan-2-ol
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of room temperature Propan-2-ol. Mix by inverting a few times. Leave at room temperature for 15-20 mins.

steps.title: Pellet the precipitated RNA by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Pellet the precipitated RNA by centrifugation at 12,000 g for 15 mins. Take note of the side of the tube where the pellet will be.

steps.title: Note the pellet will appear clear...
steps.instruction: Note the pellet will appear clear and glassy after the supernatant has been removed. Carefully remove the supernatant and wash the pellet with 500μl 70% ice cold ethanol. Be very carefull not to dislodge the pellet. It will appear white and more visable after the ethanol wash. Spin at 12,000g for 2 mins. Repeat this step one more time. (i.e. 2 ethanol washes) Carefully remove all of the ethanol.

steps.title: Allow the pellet to dry with...
steps.instruction: Allow the pellet to dry with the tube upside down on tissue at room temperature for 5-30 mins.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in RNase free...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in RNase free water in an appropriate volume (usually 30-50μl) Assess the RNA quantity and integrity using the NanoDrop and Fragment Analyzer.
